data.title: Sumida Finalizes Terms for 2026 Share-Based Stock Option Remuneration
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sumida Corporation ( (JP:6817) ) has shared an announcement.

Sumida Corporation has set the detailed terms for its fiscal 2026 share remuneration-type stock options, finalizing allocations for directors and employees of its subsidiaries as well as for its own executive officers. The scheme will grant 5,677 option units corresponding to 567,700 ordinary shares to 41 subsidiary directors and employees, and 1,468 units corresponding to 146,800 ordinary shares to four executive officers, at a payment price of ¥100,900 per unit.

The stock options form part of Sumida’s share-based compensation framework designed to tie management and staff incentives more closely to shareholder value. For executive officers, the monetary amount due on the options will effectively be settled through an offset of remuneration rights and payment obligations under Japan’s Companies Act, underscoring the company’s continued reliance on equity-linked pay to align leadership interests with long-term corporate performance.

More about Sumida Corporation

Sumida Corporation is a Japan-based manufacturer listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market under securities code 6817. The company operates in the electronics sector, with a focus on components and related technologies, and manages a network of subsidiaries whose directors, employees and executive officers participate in its equity-based compensation programs.

Average Trading Volume: 297,677

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen43.88B

data.title: Advance Create Boosts Monthly Insurance Sales and Marketing Efficiency in March 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Advance Create Co., Ltd. ( (JP:8798) ) has issued an announcement.

Advance Create Co., Ltd. reported that in March 2026, face-to-face insurance sales rose 30% from February but fell 11% year on year, while collaborative sales surged 43% month on month and 33% versus a year earlier. Non-face-to-face sales increased 17% from the prior month but declined 40% year on year, resulting in total annualized new premiums rising 32% month on month yet slipping 5% from March 2025.

Marketing performance showed notable improvement, as the number of customers acquired grew 12% from the previous month and 59% year on year, while cost per acquisition fell 12% month on month and 40% from a year earlier. These trends suggest the company’s next-generation, data-driven marketing is enhancing sales efficiency and could strengthen its competitive position in Japan’s insurance distribution market.

More about Advance Create Co., Ltd.

Advance Create Co., Ltd. operates an insurance agency business in Japan, selling insurance products through face-to-face consultations, collaborative channels, and non-face-to-face methods such as mail order and online completion. The company focuses on acquiring new customers efficiently, measuring performance through annualized premiums for new policies and marketing metrics such as cost per acquisition.

Average Trading Volume: 199,036

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: Yen6.61B

data.description:

Iperionx Limited ( (AU:IPX) ) has shared an update.

IperionX Limited has notified the market of a change in its issued capital following the lapse of certain performance rights. A total of 400,000 IPXAL performance rights ceased on April 23, 2026, after the specified conditions for converting these rights into securities were not satisfied or became incapable of being met.

The lapse of these conditional rights effectively reduces the pool of potential future equity that could have been created upon their vesting. This change may slightly affect future dilution expectations for existing shareholders, while signaling that the performance or vesting hurdles tied to these rights were not achieved within the required timeframe.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Sparebank 1 Ostlandet ( (DE:2SF) ).

SpareBank 1 Østlandet has scheduled its first-quarter 2026 results presentation for Friday 8 May at 08:15 at SpareBank 1 Markets’ offices in Oslo, where Group CEO Klara-Lise Aasen and CFO Geir-Egil Bolstad will present the figures. The event underscores the bank’s ongoing transparency with investors and analysts, with both in-person attendance and a Norwegian-language webcast available via the bank’s investor pages.

The hybrid format, including opportunities to submit questions during the live webcast, is designed to maximize accessibility for stakeholders who cannot be physically present. By formally flagging the event under Norwegian securities disclosure rules, the bank signals the importance of the upcoming quarterly update for market participants monitoring its performance and regional banking trends.

More about Sparebank 1 Ostlandet

SpareBank 1 Østlandet is a Norwegian regional savings bank operating within the SpareBank 1 alliance, offering retail and corporate banking services with a focus on eastern Norway. The bank provides traditional lending, savings, and financial advisory products, serving both private customers and businesses as part of Norway’s cooperative banking sector.

data.title: Nippon Sharyo Earnings Beat Forecasts With Strong Profit Upside
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Central Japan Railway Company ( (JP:9022) ) has provided an announcement.

Central Japan Railway Company reported that its subsidiary Nippon Sharyo, Ltd. posted full-year results for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026 that significantly exceeded prior forecasts, although the impact on the parent’s consolidated earnings is described as immaterial. The announcement underscores stronger-than-expected performance at the railcar maker, with consolidated net sales and all profit lines surpassing projections by around 2% for revenue and roughly 40% or more for operating, ordinary, and attributable profit, as well as notable upside on a non-consolidated basis, signaling improved profitability and operational efficiency at the subsidiary level.

Nippon Sharyo’s consolidated net sales reached ¥99.97 billion versus a forecast of ¥98.00 billion, while operating profit climbed to ¥11.62 billion compared with ¥8.20 billion projected. Profit attributable to owners of parent rose to ¥11.66 billion, up about 46% from the forecast, and earnings per share jumped to ¥808.18, also marking a substantial improvement from the previous fiscal year; similar positive variances were recorded in the company’s non-consolidated results, indicating a broad-based earnings outperformance relative to both guidance and prior-year levels.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9022) stock is a Hold with a Yen4200.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Central Japan Railway Company stock, see the JP:9022 Stock Forecast page.

More about Central Japan Railway Company

Central Japan Railway Company operates Japan’s Tokaido Shinkansen and related railway services, positioning it as a core transport infrastructure provider in one of the country’s most important economic corridors. Nippon Sharyo, its consolidated subsidiary, is a railcar and rolling stock manufacturer listed on the Tokyo and Nagoya exchanges, supplying equipment and services to the rail industry.

Average Trading Volume: 2,601,344

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen3857.9B

data.title: Nichimo Cuts Profit Outlook Despite Higher Sales on Marine Product Headwinds
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NICHIMO CO., LTD. ( (JP:8091) ) has issued an announcement.

Nichimo has revised its full-year consolidated forecast for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026, raising projected net sales from ¥135 billion to ¥139 billion but cutting operating profit, ordinary profit and profit attributable to owners of parent by 14–17%. Earnings per share are now expected at ¥270.64, down from a previous forecast of ¥300.03 and below last year’s ¥320.06, even as revenue is set to exceed the prior year’s ¥133.9 billion.

The downgrade reflects a slump in the surimi segment of its Food Business, higher costs for imported marine products amid adverse exchange rates, and production disruptions from a fire at a fish meal factory of a subsidiary. Additional weakness at subsidiaries making pollack roe and processed foods, plus unabsorbed cost increases in the Distribution Business, will drag operating profit about ¥550 million below the prior forecast, although the company is keeping its planned year-end dividend at ¥50 per share.

More about NICHIMO CO., LTD.

Nichimo Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based trading and manufacturing company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, with a strong focus on food-related businesses and marine products. The group operates Food and Distribution segments, dealing in imported marine products such as surimi and processed foods, as well as related logistics and distribution services for mass-market and industrial customers.

Average Trading Volume: 16,742

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen21.11B
